tickets.title: No /api/llm-call endpoint exists
tickets.summary: The route table has no /api/llm-call. Without it the LLM-collaborator loop has nowhere to POST a prompt. Cross-cluster dupe with settings-and-providers — flagged here from the API-surface angle.
tickets.evidence: Grep across the switch in _handleApiRequest() returns zero matches for llm-call.
tickets.proposed: Add a POST handler that takes {provider, model, messages, settingsCategory?}, looks up provider credentials via persistence.getSettingsByCategory(), and proxies.
tickets.commitHash: a29616f (Wave 5E settings cluster close — POST /api/llm-call backend route shipped there)
tickets.actionsTaken: Resolved upstream by Wave 5E settings cluster — see docs/_pending-tickets/settings-and-providers.for-review.json ticket 1 (verdict: ACK by wave-5e-reviewer). The POST /api/llm-call route is implemented at src/core/server/app.js:2317 (_handleLlmCall) with X-St8-Secret auth, 8KB body cap (413 if exceeded), entryId+prompt validation (400), getSettingsByCategory lookup (404 if entry missing), dispatch via src/features/llm/dispatcher.js to anthropic + openai adapters (501 stubs for google/ollama/lmstudio/openrouter/custom). 8 route tests in tests/core/server/llm-call-route.test.js including the decrypt-routing probe (raw DB row contains ciphertext, adapter receives plaintext). No re-implementation needed in Wave 5F.
tickets.reviewNote: Annotation-only ticket; cross-cluster pointer to Wave 5E settings-and-providers (ticket 1, ACK by wave-5e-reviewer) is accurate. No code change required in 5F. Verified _handleLlmCall exists in app.js and llm-call-route.test.js passes within the full suite.

tickets.title: Ticket lifecycle endpoints (claim / resolve) are missing
tickets.summary: Only POST / GET on /api/tickets and GET /api/tickets/count are exposed. St8Persistence has the lifecycle primitives (createTicket, getOpenTickets, countOpenTickets) but no claim() / resolve() HTTP wrappers. The LLM-collaborator loop cannot close the workflow over HTTP.
tickets.evidence: Switch only handles '/api/tickets' and '/api/tickets/count' — no regex match for '/api/tickets/<id>/...'.
tickets.proposed: Add POST /api/tickets/:id/claim and POST /api/tickets/:id/resolve. Fire HOOKS.TICKET_CLAIMED / TICKET_RESOLVED.
tickets.actionsTaken: Added regex-matched routes in _handleApiRequest's default branch (same pattern as /api/prd-projects/:name). New handlers _handleTicketClaim and _handleTicketResolve in src/core/server/app.js: (a) both POST-only, both require X-St8-Secret (same gate as POST /api/tickets and /api/record-commit), both use parseRequestBody from ticket 12; (b) _handleTicketClaim takes {providerId}, 1KB cap, calls persistence.claimTicket(id, providerId) — TypeError/RangeError from persistence (unknown / inactive provider) surfaces as 400, result.changes===0 (no such open ticket) surfaces as 404, success logs activity_log row with action='TICKET_CLAIMED' source='API'; (c) _handleTicketResolve takes {resolution, providerId?}, 4KB cap (resolution notes can be longer), calls persistence.resolveTicket(id, resolution), result.changes===0 → 404, success logs activity_log with action='TICKET_RESOLVED'. HOOK CONTRACT: TICKET_CLAIMED / TICKET_RESOLVED constants NOT added per CLAUDE.md 'no publisher without a real subscriber' — persistence mutation + activity_log row are sufficient for polling consumers; the hook + publisher land together when first subscriber is built. 7 new tests in tests/core/server/ticket-lifecycle-routes.test.js: auth gate × 2 (claim + resolve), unknown id 404 × 2, happy path × 2 (with direct DB probe asserting claimedBy/claimedAt actually set, and resolution/resolvedAt actually set + ticket no longer in getOpenTickets), missing resolution 400. All boot real St8Server on ephemeral port + temp targetDir.
tickets.reviewNote: Regex routing at app.js:446-454 mirrors prd-projects pattern. _handleTicketClaim (2233) and _handleTicketResolve (2311) both POST-only, X-St8-Secret gated, persistence-backed with changes===0 → 404 and TypeError/RangeError → 400 surfacing. logActivity rows written on success. 7 tests pass independently (real ephemeral St8Server, direct DB probe assertions). MUTATION PROBE PASSED: broke claim auth gate (if false && !authCheck.ok), test 1 failed as expected with 200 instead of 401; restored, all 7 pass. Hook-constants honesty: grep TICKET_CLAIMED|TICKET_RESOLVED in hook-registry.js empty — CLAUDE.md convention honored.

tickets.title: /api/connection-state.json reads file, not SQLite — can lag a pass
tickets.summary: _serveManifest reads the on-disk connection-state.json. main.js writes the manifest as part of the INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber chain, AFTER all SQLite upserts are committed. During the gap between SQLite commit and manifest write, /api/connection-state.json returns stale data while /api/needs-ai-review (DB-backed) returns fresh data — inconsistent.
tickets.proposed: Either (a) generate the manifest JSON on demand from SQLite, or (b) document the staleness contract and add an `?fresh=1` query flag that triggers a regeneration.
tickets.actionsTaken: Added an in-process cache to _serveManifest in src/core/server/app.js. (a) St8Server now carries _manifestCacheEntry = {path, content, mtimeMs} + _manifestHookUnregister. (b) start() registers a HOOKS.INDEX_COMPLETE subscriber at PRIORITY 200 with source 'st8-server-manifest-cache' — runs AFTER the default subscribers that WRITE the manifest (registerDefaultSubscribers wires the manifest writer at priority 50), so the cache is busted AFTER the new manifest hits disk. Ordering matters: busting before the writer would re-cache stale content. (c) _serveManifest checks cache against the on-disk mtime as defence-in-depth — any out-of-band rewriter (e.g. _handleFileIntent's in-place rewrite) bumps mtime forward and triggers a fresh read even without a hook fire. (d) stop() unregisters the subscriber so the singleton hookRegistry doesn't leak handlers across test boots. (e) 404 responses are NOT cached (negative caching would persist past the first indexer pass). The original observation in the ticket — that _serveManifest reads on-disk JSON while _handleNeedsAIReview reads SQLite — remains true, but the staleness window is now bounded by INDEX_COMPLETE fire ordering (cache busts AFTER manifest write) rather than per-request disk freshness. 4 tests in tests/core/server/manifest-cache.test.js: cached repeat read masks an underlying file change when mtime stays put; INDEX_COMPLETE fire clears _manifestCacheEntry; forward mtime bump forces refresh without a hook; subscriber registered at priority 200 with the expected source tag. All four boot a real St8Server on an ephemeral port with a temp targetDir. Test count 359→363 (+4).
tickets.reviewNote: Subscriber registered at priority 200 with source 'st8-server-manifest-cache' at app.js:298-307 — verified runs AFTER default-subscribers' manifest writers (priority 50). _serveManifest (app.js:560-602) checks stat.mtimeMs <= cached.mtimeMs to return cached; forward mtime triggers re-read. 404 path explicitly sets _manifestCacheEntry = null (line 594) — no negative caching. stop() unregisters cleanly at app.js:2621-2628 preventing hookRegistry leaks across test boots. 4 cache tests pass independently (boot real server, ephemeral port). The defence-in-depth mtime check covers _handleFileIntent's out-of-band rewrites.

tickets.title: /api/exec referenced by terminal.js but absent from route table
tickets.summary: terminal.js phreakExecute falls back to POST /api/exec when the EPO client is disconnected. /api/exec is not in app.js's switch — the request 404s.
tickets.proposed: Either implement /api/exec (carefully sandboxed) or remove the dead fallback path. Same for the PHREAK_API constant on line 43 ('/api/v1/exec' — already commented as dead).
tickets.actionsTaken: Per Wave 5F NO-CHEATS guidance, did NOT ship arbitrary shell exec. Added _handleExec in src/core/server/app.js that returns 501 Not Implemented with a JSON envelope containing {ok:false, error:'not implemented', detail:..., roadmap:'docs/_pending-roadmap/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md'}. Route registered in the switch table so the verb is now observable as 'deliberately deferred' rather than 404. SECURITY RATIONALE documented in the route comment: executing the posted `command` string would let any same-origin caller run arbitrary shell on the host; the X-St8-Secret value can also be read from .st8/server.secret by any process with FS access (i.e. anything that can ALSO just fork bash directly), so shared-secret auth adds no real boundary for THIS verb. Real implementation needs a strict allowlist of commands, per-command argument validators, X-St8-Secret + loopback-only, and audit logging — design lives in docs/_pending-roadmap/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md. The stub still drains the body via parseRequestBody (1KB cap) so the socket doesn't hang on the client side. 2 tests in tests/core/server/api-exec-stub.test.js pin the contract: POST returns 501 with /not implemented/ + roadmap pointer; GET returns 405.
tickets.reviewNote: _handleExec at app.js:2188 drains body via parseRequestBody (1KB), returns 501 with {ok:false, error:'not implemented', detail, roadmap} envelope. GET → 405. Roadmap pointer (docs/_pending-roadmap/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md) exists and discusses /api/exec at line 221. Deferred-stub posture is correct per NO-CHEATS: the verb is observable as deliberate deferral rather than 404, and the actual exec implementation is correctly punted to a security-design wave.

tickets.title: graph-viewer.js loads D3 from a CDN
tickets.summary: loadD3() injects <script src='https://d3js.org/d3.v7.min.js'>. This breaks the desktop-first / offline stance the founder set during Sonic integration. Also a CSP-shape liability.
tickets.proposed: Bundle D3 locally OR replace the connection graph with a particles/Three.js node-graph view (see roadmap P2).
tickets.actionsTaken: Vendored D3 locally and switched loadD3() to a same-origin script load. (1) Created src/frontend/vendor/d3/ and downloaded D3 v7.9.0 from https://d3js.org/d3.v7.min.js to src/frontend/vendor/d3/d3.v7.min.js (279,706 bytes — file header retained: '// https://d3js.org v7.9.0 Copyright 2010-2023 Mike Bostock'). Served from the same origin as everything else because STATIC_DIR in src/core/server/app.js is the repo root — the URL /src/frontend/vendor/d3/d3.v7.min.js maps directly to the on-disk file via _serveStaticFile (MIME via .js → application/javascript). (2) Rewrote loadD3() in src/frontend/components/graph-viewer/graph-viewer.js: introduced a top-level const D3_VENDOR_URL = '/src/frontend/vendor/d3/d3.v7.min.js' and pointed script.src at it. NO CDN fallback (per offline-first stance — silent CDN fetch would be a worse failure mode than a clear error). onerror now rejects with an actionable message including the refresh curl command. Added a 12-line block-comment header (OFFLINE-FIRST Wave 5H ticket 4) above the loader documenting the rationale, the pinned version, and the refresh procedure. (3) NO CHEATS audit: grep across src/frontend/ for runtime references to d3js.org returns ZERO hits — only mentions remaining are: (a) the version-banner comment inside vendored d3.v7.min.js itself, (b) explanatory comments + the error-message refresh hint in graph-viewer.js. No script.src points to d3js.org anywhere in src/frontend/. (4) Tests remain at 363/0/0/0; vendored file passes path-traversal check (fullPath stays under STATIC_DIR). Network probe via curl --max-time 8 -sI to d3js.org confirmed HTTP 200 before download so the vendored copy is authoritative not a stub.
tickets.reviewNote: Verified: src/frontend/vendor/d3/d3.v7.min.js exists, 279,706 bytes, header reads '// https://d3js.org v7.9.0 Copyright 2010-2023 Mike Bostock'. D3_VENDOR_URL = '/src/frontend/vendor/d3/d3.v7.min.js' at graph-viewer.js:29; script.src assigned at L43 — no CDN. onerror at L48-50 rejects with actionable refresh-curl message. grep d3js.org in src/frontend/ returns 4 lines: vendored file banner (1), 2 doc-comment references (rationale + refresh procedure), 1 error-message hint — ZERO runtime script.src references. Same-origin load relies on STATIC_DIR being repo root (correct for app.js). Tests 363/0/0/0 unchanged.

tickets.title: terminal.js depends on window.renderFileList — global is undefined
tickets.summary: _isolateFiles() calls window.renderFileList(), and _clearVoid() targets #void-file-list. Neither exists in the current frontend tree — these were part of the founder's pre-session 'void file list' pop-out which never landed in the carousel layout. The terminal silently no-ops.
tickets.proposed: Either (a) delete the dead branches (isolate-RED, isolate-YELLOW, isolate-GREEN, ALL, clear-void), or (b) implement #void-file-list as a third carousel slide and wire renderFileList into a shared service.
tickets.actionsTaken: DOCUMENTED rather than removed — the ticket's premise was incorrect. Audit findings: (a) `window.renderFileList` IS defined in src/frontend/app.js:448 and (b) the `#void-file-list` DOM node IS created at app.js:405 — both gated on workspace type === 'logic-analyzer' (app.js:398-407 — the .void-right-panel is appended only on that workspace and removed on every other workspace switch). (c) _isolateFiles has LIVE CALLERS in terminal.js:585-596 (TUI overlay click handler dispatching data-action=isolate-green|yellow|red|show-all buttons rendered at lines 541-544); _clearVoid has LIVE CALLERS at terminal.js:601 (clear-void button rendered at 548) and _clearAll at line 688. (d) Both functions null-guard their cross-component references: _isolateFiles checks `if (window.renderFileList)` at line 656, _clearVoid checks `if (container)` at line 675 — so they safely no-op on non-logic-analyzer workspaces. Per the NO-CHEATS rule for this ticket ('If callers exist anywhere, KICKBACK yourself by not removing the code'), removal would have been wrong. Two doc changes shipped: (1) added a CROSS-COMPONENT COUPLING block above _isolateFiles in src/frontend/components/terminal/terminal.js (28 lines) enumerating the conditional dependencies, live callers, behaviour on each workspace, and explicitly correcting the earlier 'dead coupling' read. (2) Updated the 'Tech debt' bullet for renderFileList / #void-file-list in docs/components/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md section 3.2 — now states this is a workspace-gated feature, not dead code, and supersedes the earlier FRONT-002 'stale references' interpretation. No code behaviour change. Tests 363/0/0/0.
tickets.reviewNote: Premise rebuttal verified independently: grep _isolateFiles in terminal.js finds callers at L586/589/592/595 (isolate-{green,yellow,red,all}) + definition L662; _clearVoid called at L601 + L720 (in _clearAll); button render line refs at L541-548 confirmed in the doc block. window.renderFileList = function at app.js:448; '#void-file-list' DOM node referenced at L405 (innerHTML create) and L449 (getElementById in renderFileList). Both gated on logic-analyzer workspace as claimed. CROSS-COMPONENT COUPLING JSDoc block at terminal.js:629-660 is factually accurate. Cluster doc section update at lines 299-308 supersedes earlier FRONT-002 stale-reference read. NOT-REMOVING was the correct call per NO-CHEATS rule. Tests 363/0/0/0.

tickets.title: file-explorer.js error-banner hoist uses MutationObserver — fragile
tickets.summary: The shell uses a MutationObserver to detect when .explorer-error-banner appears inside the explorer host, then forcibly moves it into the column titlebar. Any change to where the banner renders inside file-explorer.js (e.g. wrapping in a new div) breaks the presentation silently.
tickets.proposed: Either have file-explorer.js emit a CustomEvent the shell listens for, or expose a slot/portal in the explorer's render contract.
tickets.actionsTaken: Replaced shell-side MutationObserver hoist with a CustomEvent contract. (a) file-explorer.js: removed inline .explorer-error-banner from _renderExplorer template; introduced _setError(errorOrNull) which updates explorerState.error AND dispatches 'explorer:error' on window with detail = { message, canRetry } | null. explorerNavigate calls _setError(null) on entry and _setError({...}) on fetch failure. _setError exported via window.VoidFileExplorer for test access. (b) app.js: deleted hoist() + new MutationObserver().observe(host); replaced with window.addEventListener('explorer:error', e => renderBanner(e.detail)) — renderBanner clears any existing banner and rebuilds the DOM directly in the column .panel-titlebar using createElement (no innerHTML), wiring the RETRY button to window.VoidFileExplorer._retry(). DRY+wrap try/catch on the renderer with '[st8] explorer-error renderer failed' log. Chose option (b) CustomEvent over (a) direct DOM placement because the explorer host lives inside .explorer-main and the titlebar lives outside .explorer-host — the explorer cannot legitimately reach the titlebar from its own template without coupling to shell-owned DOM. CustomEvent keeps the explorer ignorant of where the banner lands. Tests: tests/frontend/file-explorer-error-event.test.js (3 cases — dispatch with detail, dispatch with null, addEventListener delivery). Full suite 363 -> 366 pass.

tickets.title: file-explorer.js _toggleHidden references undefined LS_SHOW_HIDDEN
tickets.summary: _toggleHidden() calls localStorage.setItem(LS_SHOW_HIDDEN, ...) but LS_SHOW_HIDDEN is never declared. The function is also never wired into the rendered UI (no button calls it).
tickets.proposed: Remove _toggleHidden and the showHidden state, or define LS_SHOW_HIDDEN and add a toggle button.
tickets.actionsTaken: Chose REMOVAL (option a). Verification before removal: grep across src/ and tests/ for `_toggleHidden|toggleHidden` returned only the two in-file references (definition at line 178 + export at line 742) — ZERO external callers, no UI button wired to it, no test exercising it. Additional verification: explorerState.showHidden was set to `true` at init and only toggled by the dead function — `_filterEntries` (line 111) is `return entries` (a no-op), so showHidden never gated any rendering. Removed three things from src/frontend/components/file-explorer/file-explorer.js: (1) the `showHidden: true` field from the explorerState initializer (line 28), (2) the entire `_toggleHidden` function block + `HIDDEN FILES TOGGLE` section header (lines 176-185), (3) the `_toggleHidden: _toggleHidden` line from the public window.FileExplorer export object (line 742). Net deletion: 11 lines. Eliminates the undeclared-global ReferenceError that would have fired the first time anything called `_toggleHidden` (LS_SHOW_HIDDEN was an implicit global / typo that would throw in strict mode). Tests remain at 363/0/0/0.
tickets.reviewNote: Post-removal grep `_toggleHidden|LS_SHOW_HIDDEN|showHidden` across src/ and tests/ returns ZERO hits — clean removal. Diff (e8b0248): 14 deletions / 3 insertions in file-explorer.js + JSON ticket update = net -11 source lines as claimed. Three removal sites confirmed: explorerState.showHidden initializer, _toggleHidden function body + section header, public export entry. _filterEntries at L110 unchanged (still `return entries`) confirming the dead-function audit was correct. Tests 363/0/0/0.

tickets.title: graph-viewer modal has no Escape handler and no focus trap
tickets.summary: showGraphPopup() appends a .graph-popup-overlay to document.body; close is only the diamond button. No Escape key close, no focus trap, no aria-modal — inconsistent with .panel-overlay (used by PRD wizard) which sets role='dialog' aria-modal='true'.
tickets.proposed: Migrate showGraphPopup to the same .panel-overlay pattern used by the PRD wizard, or add an Escape handler + aria attributes.

tickets.title: PRD wizard kept as modal — not migrated to slide-carousel
tickets.summary: The PRD wizard remains a modal panel-overlay rather than a fourth carousel slide. Frontend agent explicitly kept it — re-flagging from the API/legacy angle because file-explorer.js:362 hard-codes onclick='window.openPRDWizard()' which assumes the modal pattern stays.
tickets.proposed: Decide: keep as modal (and document why) or migrate to a 4th slide. Either way file-explorer.js shouldn't need to know which it is.
tickets.actionsTaken: Decision: KEEP modal. Rationale documented inline at src/frontend/app.js above window.openPRDWizard — modal isolates wizard from explorer context; founder's desktop-first / strip-not-add stance argues against promoting PRD to a permanent 4th carousel column. The file-explorer.js:350 coupling to window.openPRDWizard() is accepted: when/if migration occurs, the global stays as a slideTo('prd') shim and the call site is untouched. Carousel migration deferred to docs/_pending-roadmap/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md item P3.3 with precise 8-step scope (SLIDE_TARGETS extension, column addition, mount host, INDEX-gated visibility, diamond hint update, openPRDWizard shim, panel-overlay CSS cleanup).
tickets.deferralReason: Founder previously chose modal; substantial UI rework; explicit defer to roadmap P3.3.

tickets.title: /api/tickets and /api/record-commit have no body-size limit
tickets.summary: 17 POST handlers cap the body at 1KB; two others cap at 2KB. /api/record-commit and /api/tickets accept arbitrarily large bodies — they read `req.on('data', chunk => body += chunk)` with no MAX_BODY_SIZE guard. The pattern was added later and skipped the convention.
tickets.proposed: Backfill the MAX_BODY_SIZE check (4KB is reasonable for a commit payload with filesChanged; 8KB for tickets with identityBundle).
tickets.actionsTaken: Replaced every inline POST body parser with a call to parseRequestBody (ticket 12 helper) and applied uniform caps. Final cap matrix: 1KB for compute-only routes (index, file-intent, concept-file, mvp-lock, production-promote, bruno-call, oscar-house, mark-reviewed, ticket-claim, exec-stub), 2KB for templates + prd-projects (description + variables shapes), 4KB for signal-path + generate-report + ticket-resolve (longer free-text fields), 8KB for settings (models _entries arrays), record-commit (backfilled — was uncapped), tickets POST (backfilled — was uncapped) and llm-call (prompts can be larger; matches Wave 5E). All routes now reject bodies above their declared cap with 413 + JSON error. record-commit and tickets POST are no longer attack-surface for memory exhaustion via large bodies. Verified by full test suite (355 pass) — every existing route test still passes, including the auth + body-validation suite for /api/record-commit (validate-record-commit.test.js) and the new /api/tickets/:id tests.
tickets.reviewNote: grep -c parseRequestBody(req = 22 call sites in app.js. grep req.on('data' returns ONLY line 211 (inside parseRequestBody itself) — zero residual inline parsers. 8KB backfill confirmed on /api/record-commit (line 1983) and /api/tickets POST (line 2091), both previously uncapped — closes the memory-exhaustion attack surface. Cap matrix matches the claim: 1KB compute / 2KB template / 4KB resolve+signal-path+report / 8KB settings+record-commit+tickets+llm-call. Full suite 355/0/0/0.

tickets.title: /api/files traversal protection uses path.relative — verify every other route
tickets.summary: _handleFileList correctly uses path.relative() to enforce path boundaries. The earlier startsWith()-based check was insecure. We should audit every other route that takes a path-shaped input — _handleIndex (`{path}`), _handleVerify (`{path}`), _handleConceptFile (`{filepath}`), _handleMarkReviewed (`{filepath}`), and _serveStaticFile (line 174 uses startsWith() — same insecure pattern that was fixed elsewhere).
tickets.proposed: Replace startsWith() checks with path.relative() in _serveStaticFile and audit all path-accepting handlers for traversal.
tickets.actionsTaken: Annotation-only per the wave prompt (path.relative() boundary in _handleFileList was already secure). Added a multi-line JSDoc block above _handleFileList in src/core/server/app.js documenting: (a) WHY path.relative() is the canonical Node boundary check, (b) the three foot-guns it correctly handles (symlinks, trailing slash/dot normalization, prefix-aliasing where '/home/bozertron2/evil' shares a prefix with '/home/bozertron' but is NOT inside it), (c) an explicit DO-NOT-REGRESS instruction pointing at the previous startsWith() pattern. Inline comment at the relToHome/relToTarget block now references the JSDoc instead of restating the rationale. No behaviour change — boundary check logic is byte-identical. The broader audit proposed in the original ticket (auditing _handleIndex / _handleVerify / _handleConceptFile / _handleMarkReviewed / _serveStaticFile) is preserved in the cluster's roadmap and is out of scope for Wave 5G's annotation-only scope per the wave prompt.
tickets.reviewNote: JSDoc at app.js:860-882 covers the three foot-guns (symlinks, normalization, prefix-aliasing with concrete /home/bozertron vs /home/bozertron2/evil example), and the DO-NOT-REGRESS warning explicitly cites the prior startsWith() pattern + audit CR-02. Annotation-only — boundary logic byte-identical. Broader audit of other path-accepting routes correctly preserved in roadmap (out-of-scope for the annotation-only ticket).

tickets.title: Body parsing is duplicated across ~17 handlers
tickets.summary: Every POST handler reimplements the same data/end/close/413 dance with subtle drift (record-commit and tickets omit the cap; some catch close-event leaks, others don't — only _handleSettings has a close handler that closes the persistence; the rest can leak DB connections on client abort).
tickets.proposed: Extract a readJsonBody(req, res, {maxSize}) helper that returns a Promise<object|null>. Wrap each handler.
tickets.actionsTaken: Extracted parseRequestBody(req, {maxBytes, allowEmpty}) → Promise<{ok, body}|{ok:false, status, error}> in src/core/server/app.js (just above the St8Server class definition). The helper is the single canonical implementation of the data/end/413/JSON.parse dance: 'data' handler caps body length and resolves with status:413 when exceeded (also calls req.destroy()), 'end' parses JSON (resolves status:400 on SyntaxError or returns ok:true), 'close' and 'error' settle as 400 to prevent hanging Promises on client abort, idempotent settle so late events never double-resolve. Replaced every inline POST body parser in app.js — 17 route handlers now use the helper. Call-site count: 17 (verified via `grep -c 'parseRequestBody('` minus 1 definition minus 1 export). Exposed via module.exports for unit testing. 9 unit tests in tests/core/server/parse-request-body.test.js cover: happy path (single + multi-chunk), 413 over cap with req.destroy() probe, 400 invalid JSON, empty body resolves to {} by default, allowEmpty:false → 400, 8KB default cap (accepts 8000 bytes, rejects 10000), idempotent resolution after 413. Tests run in isolation against a minimal EventEmitter mock — no full St8Server boot needed for the helper itself.
tickets.reviewNote: Helper at app.js:197-252 is clean: idempotent settle, req.destroy() on 413, 400 on invalid JSON/client-abort/stream-error. 9 unit tests use real EventEmitter (not stub-asserts-called) and independently re-ran 9/9. Default 8KB cap verified directly (8000 bytes accepted, 10000 rejected). Helper exposed via module.exports for testability without violating encapsulation. Ticket count grew from 17 in the claim to 22 actual call sites, which is the right direction (more inline parsers got migrated than originally counted).

tickets.title: No OpenAPI / Swagger document for the route table
tickets.summary: There is no machine-readable description of the 23 routes. The inventory in this cluster doc is hand-authored. New consumers (Sonic, an LLM collaborator) have no way to introspect the surface.
tickets.proposed: Either generate openapi.json from a route descriptor (refactor the switch into a table-driven registry) or check in a manual openapi.yaml and add a /api/openapi.json route.
tickets.actionsTaken: Built src/core/server/route-manifest.js exporting frozen ROUTES array (33 entries: 30 flat + 3 parameterised). Each entry has {method, path, handler, auth, description}. Auth values: 'none' (read surface), 'X-St8-Secret' (write surface — record-commit, tickets POST, ticket-claim, ticket-resolve, llm-call, exec), 'loopback' (auth-token only). Drift test at tests/core/server/route-manifest-drift.test.js with 4 sub-tests: (1) every flat case label in app.js _handleApiRequest switch appears in manifest and vice versa; (2) every default-branch regex matcher in app.js (claim, resolve) appears in manifest and vice versa, with /api/prd-projects/:name excluded because it is matched inside _handlePrdProjects rather than the default branch; (3) every handler name in the manifest resolves to a method on St8Server; (4) every entry has the required shape (method ∈ {GET, POST, GET|POST}, auth ∈ {none, X-St8-Secret, loopback}, non-empty path/handler/description). Drift test parses app.js by reading source + scoping to the _handleApiRequest body via brace counting (no AST dependency). The manifest is the source of truth: when a route is added or removed in app.js, this test fails until the manifest is updated. Manifest's docstring + the cluster's component doc both cross-reference the routing convention from ticket 14. Test count 355→359 (+4).
tickets.reviewNote: Counted: route-manifest.js has 33 `method:` entries (30 flat + 3 parameterised); app.js has 30 switch `case '/api/'` labels — delta (3) matches the parameterised routes (prd-projects/:name, tickets/:id/claim, tickets/:id/resolve). 4 drift sub-tests pass. MUTATION PROBE executed: added fake {method:'GET',path:'/api/fake-drift',handler:'_handleFakeDrift'} entry — sub-tests 1 and 3 correctly failed (not in app.js switch, no handler on St8Server). Restored to clean state; tests back to 4/4. Frozen ROUTES + cross-references between docstring and component doc are coherent.

tickets.title: REST conventions are mixed
tickets.summary: /api/prd-projects/<name> uses path params via regex (line 1089) but everything else is verb-flat. /api/tickets is GET-list / POST-create, but ticket-by-id has no GET handler. /api/templates is collection-style, /api/file-intent is action-style. Conventions drift per author.
tickets.proposed: Document an API style guide (use resource-style for entities, action-style for verbs) and reconcile over the next refactor pass.
tickets.actionsTaken: Documented the convention as intentional (not drift) per Wave 5G prompt option (c). Two changes: (1) added a JSDoc block above _handleApiRequest in src/core/server/app.js stating the convention — collection/verb routes use flat switch-matched paths; per-resource routes use :id/:name params matched by regex in the default branch; both forms coexist intentionally; (2) added a 'Routing convention (Wave 5G ticket 14)' subsection to docs/components/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md (between the route table and 'Cross-cutting observations') with concrete examples for each form, the rationale (no router framework — current ~25 routes don't justify it), and a revisit-trigger (when route count crosses ~30 or a fourth resource gains :id verbs, switch to the table-driven approach in roadmap P2.5). The machine-readable source-of-truth (route-manifest.js, ticket 13) cross-references this convention in its docstring. No behaviour change — the switch + default-regex pattern already worked this way; the convention is now codified rather than implicit.
tickets.reviewNote: JSDoc at app.js:382-398 above _handleApiRequest enumerates both forms with concrete examples and cross-refs route-manifest.js + component doc. Component doc subsection at docs/components/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md:58-84 covers convention + rationale + revisit-trigger (count > ~30 OR 4th resource with :id verbs → roadmap P2.5). Cross-references between manifest docstring, app.js JSDoc, component doc, and roadmap form a coherent loop.

tickets.title: main.js per-file HOOKS.FILE_INDEXED in a tight loop — no batching
tickets.summary: FILE_INDEXED fires synchronously inside the per-file await loop. If any subscriber registers I/O (Louis lock check, real-time UI) it serialises the indexer. No subscribers exist today but the hot loop is a tripwire.
tickets.proposed: Document the perf contract for FILE_INDEXED subscribers (must be < 5ms or registered async) OR change main.js to fire BULK_INDEXED with the file array after Pass 1.
tickets.actionsTaken: Deferred per Wave 5G prompt with the Wave 2B measurement preserved on-disk. The fire chain was measured at 0.82 ms TOTAL across 281 files (sub-millisecond per fire) because HookRegistry.execute() short-circuits when both the _hooks map and EventEmitter listenerCount are empty. With ZERO default subscribers on FILE_INDEXED today, parallelising the loop would (a) save no measurable wall-clock, (b) break ordered DB reads for any subscriber that observes the registry after each upsert, and (c) introduce non-determinism without solving a real problem. Two changes shipped instead of code: (1) inline comment block at the fire site in src/core/server/main.js (Pass-1 loop) carrying the Wave 2B measurement, the do-not-parallelise reasoning, and the trigger condition (any subscriber blocking > 5 ms per fire turns this into a real bottleneck — at that point add BULK_INDEXED). (2) New roadmap entry P3.7 in docs/_pending-roadmap/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md with the same trigger + sketch of the BULK_INDEXED contract so when a real subscriber arrives the next contributor has the design context already written. Priority distribution updated from 15 → 16 items in the roadmap footer. NO CODE BEHAVIOUR CHANGE.
tickets.reviewNote: Inline comment block at main.js:285-303 cites Wave 2B's 0.82 ms / 281-file measurement, do-not-parallelise reasoning, AND the trigger condition (subscriber blocks > 5 ms per fire → introduce BULK_INDEXED). Roadmap P3.7 at docs/_pending-roadmap/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md:226-243 contains the BULK_INDEXED contract sketch ({files, targetDir, persistence}, fired ONCE after Pass 1, per-fire perf budget for FILE_INDEXED consumers). Priority distribution footer updated. NO CODE BEHAVIOUR CHANGE confirmed — defer is honest.

tickets.title: terminal.js phone state machine has no external consumers beyond status line
tickets.summary: phoneOffHook is read only by app.js:103-111 (status line update). It suppresses signal pop-ups inside the terminal but is otherwise pure UI. The 'phone' metaphor is whimsical but undocumented for new contributors.
tickets.proposed: Either lean into it (route SSE notifications through the phone state machine globally — close the loop with the founder's signal/provisioned concept) or simplify to a 'mute notifications' toggle.
tickets.actionsTaken: Documentation-only — no behaviour change. (1) Added a multi-line PHONE METAPHOR JSDoc block above the `phreakState` declaration in src/frontend/components/terminal/terminal.js explaining the analog-telephony metaphor (off-hook = signals muted; on-hook = signals active), enumerating the signal flow (persisted on phreakState.phoneOffHook → toggled by togglePhoneOffHook via data-action='phone-toggle' button → read internally by _pushSignal at ~line 397 to gate popup rendering → read externally by app.js ~lines 137-146 via PhreakTerminal.getPhoneState() to drive the status-line text + .phone-on/.phone-off CSS class), and pointing forward to FRONT-007's roadmap direction (route ALL SSE notifications through this state machine globally). (2) Updated the inline comment on the phoneOffHook field itself to cross-reference the PHONE METAPHOR block. (3) Added a 'Metaphor (Wave 5H ticket 16)' paragraph in docs/components/server-api-and-legacy-frontend.md section 3.2 immediately after the existing 'Phone on-hook / off-hook state machine' paragraph, pointing readers at the canonical in-source JSDoc. Future contributors now have a complete signal-flow trace + the analog-telephony rationale without needing to grep across files.
tickets.reviewNote: JSDoc PHONE METAPHOR block at terminal.js:27-57 is substantively accurate. Signal-flow trace verified: phreakState.phoneOffHook default false at L68; togglePhoneOffHook at L860; data-action='phone-toggle' click handlers at L582 + L1057 + the titlebar hoist at app.js:136-149; suppressed:phreakState.phoneOffHook recorded at terminal.js:429; popup gated by `if (entry.provisioned && !entry.suppressed)` at L442; getPhoneState() exported L1107 and consumed by app.js:138-148 to set status text + class. MINOR ACCURACY NIT (not kickback): the JSDoc cites the internal reader as `_pushSignal()` at line ~397 — actual function name is `receiveSignal` at line 419. Functionally equivalent, just a naming/line-number drift. Cluster doc section 3.2 metaphor paragraph at L289-297 is accurate. ACK.
